title: Plinia hatschbachii
taxonomy: Plinia hatschbachii is classified within the family Myrtaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This plant is further classified within the order Myrtales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is also known as the flowering plants or angiosperms. The family Myrtaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Plinia hatschbachii is a species of flowering plant that is endemic to Brazil.

intro: Plinia hatschbachii, also known as the 'Brazilian guava', is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Plinia hatschbachii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Plinia hatschbachii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues.
